diagrams.title: Hierarchy of Walk Types
diagrams.description: Shows the containment relationship from most general (walk) to most specific (cycle)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding progressive constraints in graph traversal concepts

diagrams.title: Isomorphic Graphs Example
diagrams.description: Two different drawings of C4 (cycle with 4 vertices) showing they represent the same graph
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing that isomorphic graphs can look different but have identical structure

diagrams.title: Spanning vs Induced Subgraph
diagrams.description: Original K4 complete graph with highlighted spanning subgraph (all 4 vertices, 3 edges forming tree) and induced subgraph (2 vertices with edge between them)
elements: Subset of vertices with all connecting edges shown
diagrams.purpose: Distinguishing between spanning and induced subgraph types

diagrams.title: Walk, Path, Circuit Examples
diagrams.description: Sample graph showing examples of walk (with repeats), path (no repeats), and circuit (returns to start)
diagrams.purpose: Concrete examples of each traversal type on same graph
